chart_checklist_amount_description: Displays the number of inspections performed by each user in the selected period. Use filters to see more detailed information.
chart_claim_reasons_description: The diagram shows the rating of reasons why claims were created. It can help you identify processes that require additional attention.
chart_claim_values_description: The diagram shows rating by values that were used when creating photo reports. It can be used to identify values that require additional attention.
chart_claims_quantity_description: Shows the number of claims in each {{shop_l_p}}. The diagram helps to identify the most problematic {{shop_l_v_m}} that require additional attention.
chart_company_condition_description: Displays the scores with color differentiation. Values in rows and columns are editable. For example, helps to find the weakest process by store or the weakest store by process.
chart_grade_and_issue_dynamics_descripti: When you select a specific process, the average process rating and the number of issues by day in the selected period will be displayed. Use filters to get more detailed information about a point or template.
chart_grade_divisions_description: Displays the average score in the {{division_l_p}} for inspections performed in the selected period. Filter the data by pattern to get more detailed information on them in each {{division_l_p}}.
chart_grade_processes_description: Displays the average score of each process over the selected period. Select a specific process or use filters to get more detailed information about this process at a point of interest or in checklists with this process.
chart_grade_regions_description: Displays the average score in the {{region_l_p}} for inspections performed in the selected period. Filter the data by pattern to get more detailed information on them in each {{region_l_p}}.
chart_grade_shops_description: Displays the average score for all processes in the {{shop_l_p}} for the selected parameters. Select a {{shop_l_i}} to see information on it in all charts. Use filters to get more detailed information about the situation in the {{shop_l_p}}.
chart_grade_supervisors_description: Displays the average score of each inspector for the selected period. Select an inspector to see information on them in all charts. Use filters to get more detailed information about the inspector.
chart_grade_templates_description: Displays the average score for each template in the selected period. Select a template to see information on it in all charts. Use filters to get more detailed information about the template at a specific point.
chart_interval_description: The quality shows the number of inspections with one estimate or another in the context of the compared periods. By hovering over the scale, you can compare the number of inspections with this rating in the current period with the number of inspections with the same rating in the previous period (for example, the current week and the previous). The farther to the right the distribution is shifted (the more it tends to the highest estimate), the better.

checklist_template_import_expanded_weigh: When the weighted calculation mode is extended, the final audit scoring is displayed as a number. In this calculation mode, the answer to the question is from 1 to 5.
checklist_template_import_percentage_des: In the percentage calculation mode, the final score for the test is displayed as a percentage. When answering Yes to the checklist question, the system assigns 100% to the question. When the answer is No it is 0%. When the answer is Average — 50%.
checklist_template_import_summary_desc: In the consolidated calculation mode, the total score of the audit is displayed as a number - the sum of weights of all the questions to which the answers "Yes" or "Average" were given. In case of Yes answer to the checklist question, the system assigns the full weight to the question. In case of No answer it counts as 0. In case of Average answer, it is half the question weight.
checklist_template_import_weighted_desc: If the calculation mode is weighted, the final score is displayed as a number. When answering Yes to a checklist question, the system assigns the maximum possible value of 5 to the question. Answer No — 0. Answer Average — 2.5.

guide_business_line_desc: Departments show the organization structure of the company. For example (HR, Quality Control, Security Service). And they are used to determine the powers of users. For easy customization, add your users to the existing BD 'Operations'
guide_checklist_desc: In order for the checklist to be ready for passage, it is required to assign to it a group of users who will have access to it.
guide_checklist_title: Assign a checklist to user groups or departments
guide_clusters_desc: {{region_u_i}} - the totality of its constituent {{shop_l_p_m}}. When you link the checking level '{{region_u_i}}' to a certain {{region_l_d}}, all the {{shop_l_p_m}} in the region are available for inspections.
guide_divisions_desc: {{division_u_i}} - a set of its constituent {{region_l_r_m}}. When linking the inspection level of the '{{division_u_i}}' to a certain {{division_l_d}}, all {{shop_l_p_m}} entering the {{region_l_i_m}} of the given {{division_l_r}} are available for inspections.
guide_group_desc: Groups are created to assign users access to checklists and reports. Users who are not added to the corresponding group will not be able to see the checklists.
guide_process_wizard_desc: Questions are grouped into groups corresponding to certain areas of the trading floor or company process (for example, personnel, security or documents). Processes are combined in a hierarchy of blocks or sub-blocks, which is used in the reporting and the work of analytical panels.
guide_shops_desc: {{shop_u_i}} - the main object system used for inspection.
guide_template_wizard_desc: On the checklist template, checklists are formed. The checklist template is formed from questions that may be in different processes. The sequence of questions in the checklist is specified when creating the template. Also, in the template properties, the rules for calculating the scoring and sending notifications are set.
guide_users_desc: User is a registered employee. It is created for the employee to get access to the system's functionality. On the 'Users' tab, the level of user access to the shops is set.

users_import_manual_auth_type: Authorization type is an optional field. It can either be Local or LDAP (if LDAP authorization was enabled, a user would use his Windows password to log into the system). If no authorization type was specified, the Local authorization type will be set by default.
users_import_manual_bd: Department is a required field. The department should be selected from the list. Values of this field cannot be changed manually. Users with this field left empty will not be imported into the system.
users_import_manual_email: E-mail is an optional field. All system notifications (e.g. notifications on assigned tasks) are to be sent on emails specified in users’ accounts so we suggest you fill it for all of your users.
users_import_manual_ldap: LDAP login is only required if the LDAP authorization type was selected.
users_import_manual_level: Level is a required field. The user level is to be selected from the list. Values of this field cannot be changed manually. Users with this field left empty will not be imported into the system.
users_import_manual_login: Login is a required field. Login is unique for each user and must contain at least 3 characters.
users_import_manual_name: Last name and First name are optional fields. Nonetheless, it is recommended to fill them so that users can be identified easier.
users_import_manual_objects:
The Object field is required for users of all levels except for the Company. It should be left blank for users of the Company level.
For users of the {{division_u_i}} or {{region_u_i}} level at least one ID of an object of the corresponding level must be specified.
For users of the “Object” (e.g. store, station, etc.) level at least one SAP number of a corresponding level object must be specified.
If several objects should be assigned to a user, object IDs/SAP numbers should be separated by a semicolon (;).
Please keep these limitations in mind when filling the Objects field:
- Users with both “Active user” and “Store manager” roles assigned should never share the same object ID. Such users will not be imported into the system.
- If the level of the specified object does not match the user level, the user will be imported into the system without such an object assigned.
- If one of the specified IDs/SAP numbers does not exist in the system, the user will be imported into the system without such an object assigned.
- If none of the specified objects was assigned to the user due to the reasons listed above, such a user will not be imported into the system.
users_import_manual_password: Password is an optional field. It should contain at least 6 characters. If this field was left unfilled, the default password from Import settings will be used instead.
users_import_manual_roles:
Active user, Administrator, Inspector, Store manager, Can plan inspections of employees, Can edit surveys
Roles are optional fields. Filling these fields, either the Yes or No value should be selected from the list. If any field was left empty, the No value is set by default.

task_import_application_data_hint:
Download the XLS file template and fill it with data.
Do not modify the template! Otherwise, an error will occur when uploading the file, and task creation will be impossible.
The '{{shop_u_i}} Number' column is optional. If the '{{shop_u_i}} Number' column is empty on all sheets and rows, a task will be created for each {{shop_l_r}} selected below in the 'Select Performers' section. In this case, all inspection items from the file will be included in the task for each {{shop_l_r}}.
If the '{{shop_u_i}} Number' column is filled in on all sheets and rows, a task will only be created for the {{shop_l_v_m}} specified in the file, and for each {{shop_l_r}}, only the inspection items specified for that {{shop_l_r}} will be included in the task.
If the '{{shop_u_i}} Number' column is filled in for some rows and not for others, an error will occur when uploading the file, and task creation will be impossible.

ai_photoreport_description_hint:
To create a high-quality task for the AI assistant, you can follow this structure:
1. Start with the task
Describe in simple words what exactly needs to be checked in the photo: for example, “check if there is a price tag”, “make sure the display matches the plan”.
2. Name the object of verification
Specify which item or area is in question: product on the shelf, promotional material, checkout area, refrigerator, etc.
3. Add a presence/absence criterion
Formulate whether the object should be in the photo or, on the contrary, should not be there.
4. Clarify the location or context
If the place where the object is located is important, add it: “on the middle shelf”, “to the left of the checkout”, “in the center of the showcase”.
The final request may look like this: "I want to check if there is a promotional poster in the photo. It should be located to the right of the entrance."

projects_charts_rating_health_descriptio: This indicator shows how closely actual execution matches the original plan. Only tasks from the baseline plan that are still part of the project are included in the calculation, and the assessment is based on effort rather than quantity, so larger tasks have a greater impact on project health. Each task is assigned a status: on schedule, with minor delay, or with critical delay. The boundary between minor and critical delay is defined by two thresholds — number of days and percentage of the task duration. The higher the share of effort for tasks completed on time or with minor delays, and the lower the share of critically overdue tasks, the higher the project health.
projects_charts_rating_critical_delay_de: A delay that exceeds both thresholds — by number of days and by percentage of the task duration. If only one of the thresholds is exceeded, the task is considered to have a minor delay. If neither threshold is exceeded, the task is not considered delayed.
projects_charts_rating_small_delay_descr: A delay where only one of the established thresholds is exceeded — either by number of days or by percentage of the task duration. If both thresholds are exceeded, the delay is considered critical. If neither is exceeded, the task is not considered delayed.
projects_charts_health_description: Project health is an indicator calculated based on the number of tasks, their duration, and their timely execution. In the right filter panel, you can configure the threshold at which a task delay is considered critical. The threshold can be configured both in days and as a percentage. Only if both thresholds are exceeded will the delay be considered critical. You can also adjust how strongly different types of delays affect project health.
